stem: A kite is flying at a height of 30 feet directly above a point on the ground. The kite string makes a 37-degree angle with the ground. Given that sin 37° ≈ 0.6 and cos 37° ≈ 0.8, what is the approximate length of the kite string, in feet?
explanation: The kite string is the hypotenuse of a right triangle. The height of the kite (30 feet) is the side opposite the 37° angle at the ground. Since sin 37° = opposite / hypotenuse, we have 0.6 = 30 / hypotenuse. Solving: hypotenuse = 30 / 0.6 = 50 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Multiplied the height by sin instead of dividing. The student computed 30 × 0.6 = 18
distractorRationale.B: Multiplied the height by cos instead of dividing by sin. The student computed 30 × 0.8 = 24
distractorRationale.C: Used tan 37° ≈ 0.75 instead of sin 37°. The student divided the height by tan: 30 / 0.75 = 40
distractorRationale.D: Correct: Recognized that sin relates the opposite side (height) to the hypotenuse (string), then solved hypotenuse = 30 / sin 37° = 30 / 0.6 = 50

imageDescription: A right triangle with a stick figure at the bottom-left holding a string. The string (hypotenuse) extends up and to the right to a kite icon. The vertical leg is labeled 30 ft (height), the angle at the ground between the string and the horizontal is labeled 37°, and the hypotenuse is labeled with a question mark. The right angle is at the bottom-right where the vertical height meets the ground.
